P1-LAG-SAMPLE-CLOCK-CONTRACT.approach: Fetch (event_id, created_at, processed_at) for processed event_id set via supabase-js .from('finance_outbox').select(...).in(...); subtract Date.parse(processed_at) - Date.parse(created_at) in TS. Both timestamps DB-clock stamped (created_at by producer trigger, processed_at by rpc_process_*_projection — both via Postgres now()).

literal_sql_deviation.implementation: Date.parse subtraction in TS (TS-side, but operating on two DB-clock-stamped strings)
literal_sql_deviation.rationale: supabase-js cannot express the SQL expression in .select(); adding an RPC was out of scope for WS1 (gate is type-check, not schema-validation). Semantic equivalence holds — both timestamps originate from Postgres now(); no app-server clock participates.
literal_sql_deviation.orchestrator_decision_needed: Accept the semantic-equivalence deviation OR direct WS2 to add an rpc_get_outbox_lag_samples alongside the cleanup migration. Recommendation: accept (no production correctness impact; devils-advocate may revisit at Phase 4).

P1-LAG-SAMPLE-CLOCK-CONTRACT.exclusion_by_construction: Duplicate / skipped / failed / claim-error / auth-fail outcomes are never pushed to classAProcessedEventIds (Class A) or processedEventIds (operational); they never appear in the .in(event_id, ...) lag query. Verified by 'exclusion by construction' test in outbox-relay-log-emission.test.ts.

P1-BACKLOG-CLAIMABILITY-DEFINITION.ledger_predicate_parity: MATCH — log-line uses fact_class='ledger' AND processed_at IS NULL, matching rpc_claim_class_a_outbox_batch verbatim.
P1-BACKLOG-CLAIMABILITY-DEFINITION.operational_predicate_parity: DRIFT — rpc_claim_operational_outbox_batch (20260521022656) includes event_type IN ('grind.observed','fill.recorded','credit.recorded') filter that the EXEC-SPEC canonical predicate table did not enumerate. Per audit-patch resolution rule ('log-line predicate follows the RPC source of truth'), both operational.claimable and operational.dead_letter log-line predicates now include the same event_type whitelist. Implementation: OPERATIONAL_EVENT_TYPES constant in outbox-consumer.ts; applied in collectOutboxBacklog Promise.all queries.

artifacts.prd_overview_summary: Phase 2.5 = closing slice of Wave 2. Three deliverables: (1) structured relay-cycle + cleanup-cycle log lines for substrate observability, (2) cron-driven 7-day retention via SECURITY DEFINER RPC + partial index, (3) WAVE-2-SIGN-OFF.md ratifying PROD-ANCHOR-STD-001 and reconciling residual backlog. No new UI surfaces.
artifacts.ws1_summary: WS1_LOG delivered: relay route emits one structured outbox_relay_cycle log per cycle (FR-1 authenticated, FR-2 auth_fail); GET export for Vercel cron; DB-clock derived lag samples (P1 audit honored, with TS-subtraction deviation flagged); backlog predicates follow RPC source of truth including event_type whitelist drift (P1 audit honored); HTTP response backward-compatible; type-check + lint + tests all green; 30 new tests.
artifacts.ws2_summary: WS2_RETENTION delivered: migration 20260521142441_create_rpc_cleanup_outbox_processed.sql with rpc_cleanup_outbox_processed(p_max_rows INTEGER DEFAULT 1000) RETURNS INTEGER (SECURITY DEFINER, SET search_path='', service_role-only EXECUTE, CTE-based DELETE with FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED, p_max_rows validation NULL/<1/>1000, partial index idx_finance_outbox_processed_retention ON (processed_at, event_id) WHERE processed_at IS NOT NULL); GET /api/internal/outbox-cleanup with CRON_SECRET bearer auth and structured outbox_cleanup_cycle log lines (auth_fail / ok / error variants); vercel.json daily cron entry 0 7 * * *; 11 unit tests + 10 integration tests (3 age bands, p_max_rows cap, 4 invalid-cap variants, first-run cap, EXPLAIN split assertion deterministic index-exists + predicate-alignment) + 1 advisory planner-evidence test behind RUN_PLANNER_EVIDENCE_TESTS env gate; INV-ERR-DETAILS honored via safeErrorDetails.

dev_env_finding_flagged_for_followup.finding: .env (loaded by jest.setup.js with override:false) contains REMOTE Supabase URL/keys; .env.local contains the LOCAL URL/keys. RUN_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true npx jest plainly hits the remote instance which lacks unmerged migrations and fails ALL finance_outbox-touching integration tests (verified by running existing class-a-projection.int.test.ts against the plain invocation). To run against local DB, tests must be invoked with explicit local env vars (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:54321 + LOCAL anon/service keys).
dev_env_finding_flagged_for_followup.recommended_remediation: Either (a) flip jest.setup.js to prefer .env.local over .env in test runs, or (b) document the explicit-override requirement in QA-006/QA-001 integration test playbook, or (c) introduce .env.test that holds the local URL/keys and have jest.setup.js load it first.
dev_env_finding_flagged_for_followup.rationale: WS3/WS4 are documentation workstreams that do not run integration tests, so dispatch is not blocked. Pre-merge DoD (per PRD-089 §8) must include either fix or explicit documentation.
